skill.name: three
skill.description: Three.js and WebGL adapter patterns for HyperFrames. Use when creating deterministic Three.js scenes, WebGL canvas layers, AnimationMixer timelines, camera motion, shader-driven visuals, or canvas renders that respond to HyperFrames hf-seek events.
skill.summary: Create deterministic Three.js and WebGL scenes that render from HyperFrames seek time.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution hits, but they are Markdown inline code, fenced examples, or user-run validation commands. No malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence was found. The remaining risks are low supply-chain exposure from a CDN import example and local project commands.

low_findings.title: Markdown Syntax Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick locations are Markdown code markers, inline code, and documentation examples. They do not execute by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation, and the suspicious tokens are code formatting or sample commands. No executable Ruby or shell wrapper is present.

low_findings.title: Remote CDN Import in Browser Example
low_findings.description: The example imports Three.js from jsDelivr. This is legitimate documentation, but users should consider pinned local dependencies for production.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a pinned Three.js ESM import in an example. It creates supply-chain exposure only if copied into production unchanged.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded Three.js documentation URLs are reference links. They do not send local data or credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The links point to public Three.js documentation pages. No request code or data transmission behavior appears around them.

low_findings.title: False Positive Crypto and Reconnaissance Signals
low_findings.description: No weak cryptographic algorithm or system reconnaissance behavior was found. The flagged lines contain description text and animation timing guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic API, host probing, environment inspection, or filesystem enumeration. The flagged context is rendering advice.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Run Validation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill suggests running HyperFrames validation commands. This is expected tooling guidance, but users should run it inside trusted projects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible documentation examples and are not hidden or automated. They still execute local package tooling when a user runs them.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Module Import Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The sample uses a remote ESM dependency URL. This is transparent and versioned, but production users should review dependency sourcing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The import target is an established CDN and a pinned Three.js version. The risk depends on whether users copy the pattern.

content.value_statement: Video-oriented Three.js scenes can drift when they depend on wall-clock animation loops. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to render WebGL content from HyperFrames seek time.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to wire a Three.js canvas to HyperFrames seek events.
Explains deterministic rendering from time instead of wall-clock loops.
Provides a basic WebGLRenderer, scene, camera, and mesh pattern.
Documents AnimationMixer seeking with a shared render time.
Lists validation commands for HyperFrames linting and validation.
limitations: Does not include reusable source files or packaged JavaScript utilities.
Does not load models, textures, or HDRIs for the user.
Does not replace Three.js performance tuning or asset optimization.
Requires existing HyperFrames knowledge and project setup.

use_cases.title: Create Product Spin Scenes
use_cases.description: Build deterministic product rotations that render the same frame for the same HyperFrames time.

use_cases.title: Integrate GLTF Clip Playback
use_cases.description: Seek AnimationMixer timelines directly so authored animations align with a composition timeline.

use_cases.title: Validate WebGL Video Layers
use_cases.description: Check that canvas size, pixel ratio, and render timing are suitable for repeatable video output.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Scene
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the three skill to create a simple HyperFrames Three.js canvas scene that renders from hf-seek time.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Non-Deterministic Motion
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Three.js HyperFrames scene and replace wall-clock animation logic with deterministic renderAt time logic.

prompt_templates.title: Add GLTF Timeline Seeking
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the three skill to adapt a GLTF AnimationMixer so it seeks from HyperFrames time before each render.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Render Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this WebGL HyperFrames composition for deterministic output, asset loading timing, pixel ratio stability, and validation readiness.

output_examples.input: Create a deterministic Three.js object layer for HyperFrames.
output_examples.output: The assistant describes a canvas layer, fixed renderer dimensions, a scene, a camera, and a render function driven by seek time.
The assistant identifies wall-clock timing, recommends seek-based rendering, and explains which animation loops should stop driving final output.

output_examples.input: Prepare a GLTF animation for video rendering.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends preloading assets, seeking each mixer from the same time value, and validating the composition before rendering.

best_practices: Render from HyperFrames seek time, not from Date.now or frame deltas.
Load render-critical models and textures before validation or final rendering.
Pin renderer size and pixel ratio for repeatable video frames.
anti_patterns: Do not fetch remote models or textures during seek-time rendering.
Do not leave final motion controlled by requestAnimationFrame loops.
Do not use post-processing history unless state can be reconstructed from time.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build deterministic Three.js and WebGL canvas layers for HyperFrames compositions.

faq.question: Does it include executable code files?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill with patterns and examples in SKILL.md.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why avoid requestAnimationFrame for final renders?
faq.answer: Free-running loops can produce different frames across renders. HyperFrames seek time gives repeatable output.

faq.question: Can it work with GLTF animations?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends seeking AnimationMixer timelines directly from the shared HyperFrames time.

faq.question: Is the CDN import required?
faq.answer: No. The CDN import is an example. Production projects can use local pinned dependencies.
